key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 net cash from operating activities $1,493.7M / 192.7M weighted average shares (2025 annual report MD&A)
2026 GEO guidance 510-570k; fixed conversion ratios; dividend $1.76/yr; mid-single-digit cash flow growth [Assumption] off FY2025 record

open_questions: SEC EDGAR folder not yet populated.
Q1 2026 CRA refund and Cascabel buy-back: confirm normalized owner cash if promoting overlay.
Cobre Panamá probability and terminal value are **[Assumption]**.
Synthesis weights pending human approval in `valuation.json`.
thesis: Franco-Nevada is a Toronto- and New York-listed gold-focused royalty and streaming company. **[Fact]** It earns cash from gross-metal-equivalent ounces (GEOs) on third-party mines without operating production assets or mine-level capital spending. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$1,822.8 million** (+64%); operating cash flow **$1,493.7 million** (+80%); **519,106** GEOs sold including **11,208** from Cobre Panamá (`investor-documents/ir-fnv/NR-Franco-Nevada-Reports-Record-2025-Results-vF-2026-03-10.pdf`). **[Fact]** The company reports **no debt** and **$3.1 billion** available capital at year-end 2025, rising to **$3.4 billion** at March 31, 2026 (`investor-documents/ir-fnv/NR-Franco-Nevada-Reports-Record-Q1-2026-Results-vFinal-2026-05-12.pdf`). **[Fact]** Quarterly dividend is **$0.44** per share (16% increase for 2026; **$1.76** annualized).
why_market_wrong: The market may treat Franco-Nevada as a passive gold beta at a peak multiple and underweight the portfolio option stack. **[Inference]** Record FY2025-Q1 2026 cash flows embed elevated gold, silver, and (in Q2 outlook) oil prices; a quality premium may compress if commodities normalize. **[Inference]** Cobre Panamá restart, recent acquisitions (Cascabel buy-back, Victoria Gold portfolio, Bullabulling), and operator exploration spend on the royalty book are not fully in 2026 GEO guidance. **[Inference]** Unlike operating miners, Franco-Nevada benefits from higher oil prices without proportionate cost inflation (CEO Q1 2026 remarks).
